rxn_class_summary: Ni/Fe dual-catalyzed three-component radical 1,2-acylalkylation of alkenes with acyl chlorides and C(sp3)-H alkanes via HAT and radical addition

key_intermediates.name: cyclohexyl radical (alkyl C-radical from HAT on cyclohexane)
acyl radical / Ni-acyl species from p-toluoyl chloride
alkyl-Ni intermediate after radical addition to acrylate

electron_flow: Fe/Cl-based HAT abstracts a C-H from the alkane to give an alkyl radical, which adds to the acrylate; the resulting α-ester radical is captured by a Ni-acyl species (from oxidative addition of the acyl chloride) and reductive elimination delivers the 1,2-acylalkylated product.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Desired product 4 not detected (n.d.); TEMPO-cyclohexyl adduct 47 detected by GCMS, indicating a free cyclohexyl radical intermediate

notes: Page shows only the TEMPO trapping experiment from the Mechanistic Investigations section; the trapped alkyl radical (cyclohexyl) supports a HAT-generated C(sp3) radical pathway. Standard conditions employ NiBr2·DME / dtbbpy, FeCl3·6H2O, LiCl in acetone/MeCN. Further mechanistic details (e.g., role of Ni vs Fe, acyl radical generation pathway) not visible on this page.
